assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
The intragroup dividend has resulted in a recognised monetary receivable/liability before consolidation effects are considered.
The issue is the possible use of hedge accounting for foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to a particular risk that could affect profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately recognises hedges of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: IAS 21 foreign currency accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the baseline accounting model for exchange differences on foreign currency transactions and intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements.
